published_prior_art.cite: Kuroki2013: sharp surrogate-endpoint bounds frame this as partial identification; Theorem 1 is only bounded-mixture algebra before endpoint-table compatibility

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_long_term_surrogate_v1: noisy proxy/bridge sign ambiguity, not a responder principal-stratum phase frontier

published_prior_art.cite: YinLiuGengLuo2020 and MaYinLiuGeng2021: exclusion optimality and individual surrogate paradox; no exact L_R responder frontier found, but the proposed kernel is a routine bounded-mixture refinement
VanderWeele2013: qualitative sufficient conditions for consistent surrogates; this corollary is the scalar threshold consequence of L_R

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal calls Gamma_Y an average surrogate-sufficiency margin, but in the principal-surrogate literature ACS is a CEP/principal-stratum condition; as defined here Gamma_Y is the overall endpoint ATE margin.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: A closer omitted comparator is GilbertGabrielHuang2015, which directly analyzes ACN, ACS, CEP surfaces, and surrogate-paradox consistency in the principal-stratification framework; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship unless the result is anchored against that frontier.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed average-sufficiency interpretation in the abstract and introduction does not match the formal Gamma_Y definition in Section 6, which is simply the aggregate endpoint treatment effect.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The phase threshold follows from decomposing Gamma_Y and putting nonresponder effects at their binary upper bound; the finite-table sharpness route is mostly routine mixture algebra plus a rank-stable thickening, so the kernel is field-tier at best.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Redefine the margin as the actual principal-surrogate ACS or CEP object used by Gilbert-Hudgens/VanderWeele/Gilbert-Gabriel-Huang, then restate the frontier for the responder CEP rather than for the overall endpoint ATE.

Add a strict observed-data theorem against Yin-Liu-Geng-Luo: when their exclusion criterion fails, give an explicit computable responder-effect lower bound or witness-selection algorithm and prove why it is sharper than mere existence of a paradox DGP.
Move Theorem 1 and Corollary 1 to calibration status and make the flagship claim a comparator-anchored iff or sharp-bound result involving at least two named surrogate-paradox criteria.

recommended_next_step: Do not polish this kernel as flagship; redraft around an actual ACS/CEP or observed-data exclusion frontier, or pivot.
